jokes.text: How old is that handkerchief? Well, it's not a handkerchief. It's a receipt.
jokes.setup: Someone questions what appears to be an old handkerchief
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be an ancient receipt, suggesting extreme age/frugality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with economical dialogue. Character-specific humor about elderly person keeping receipts forever.

jokes.text: I mean, technically, I'm not allowed to reserve this conference room without my husband or father's signature.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about women's progress in Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Reveals an absurdly sexist current policy contradicting her optimism
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction. Leslie's earnestness makes the absurd policy even funnier.

jokes.text: They smoked their cigars, they snapped my bra, wore mirrors on their shoes to look up my skirt.
jokes.setup: Paula describes the 'boys' club' atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Each behavior gets progressively more inappropriate and cartoonish
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure. The mirror shoes detail is absurdly specific and memorable.

jokes.text: Well, I am happy to report that it is now a pervert-free people's club. Well, except for Councilman Dexhart, who's a self-described pervert.
jokes.setup: Leslie proudly declares the council is now pervert-free
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself with a specific exception
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie optimism immediately undercut. The 'self-described' detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: All the other councilmen used to keep a calendar of my menstrual cycle.
jokes.setup: Paula continues describing past sexism
jokes.punchline: Reveals an extremely invasive and inappropriate tracking system
jokes.explanation: Shockingly specific and uncomfortable detail that's both appalling and funny. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: They dismissed me, saying that it was because it was just my time of the month. Admittedly, they were right. Because of the calendar.
jokes.setup: Paula describes being dismissed for menstrual reasons
jokes.punchline: Admits they were correct because they literally tracked it
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes the previous joke even more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: You know what, I think men are better than women. She's kidding. No, I'm not.
jokes.setup: April makes an outrageous statement, Leslie tries to cover
jokes.punchline: April doubles down with characteristic deadpan commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment. Her commitment to the bit is what makes it funny.

jokes.text: They provide for us, and we must obey them because they are our masters. Leslie, you'll never land a beau with that domineering tone.
jokes.setup: April continues her fake anti-feminist stance
jokes.punchline: Criticizes Leslie's tone as too domineering while advocating total submission
jokes.explanation: The word 'beau' and the ironic contradiction make this quintessentially April.

jokes.text: Moustache! Dumb-dumb!
jokes.setup: Kids are playing and making noise
jokes.punchline: They're calling people silly names including 'dumb-dumb'
jokes.explanation: Simple kid humor. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I borrowed some markers from Leslie and some stickers from Leslie and a Lite-Brite which I got from Andy.
jokes.setup: Ron explains where he got supplies for the kids
jokes.punchline: Andy is the source for the most childish item while Leslie provided office supplies
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - of course Andy has a Lite-Brite in his office.

jokes.text: Hey, dudettes. You stoked about the weekend? No.
jokes.setup: Ann tries to be cool with slang
jokes.punchline: Immediate flat rejection from the kids
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward Ann moment. The immediate 'No' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Oh, look at this pwetty, pwetty wittle beads. They're not infants.
jokes.setup: Ann baby-talks to the kids
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan correction that they're not babies
jokes.explanation: Ann's awkwardness with kids is well-established character comedy. Ron's correction is perfectly dry.

jokes.text: So you guys like Coldplay? You're weird.
jokes.setup: Ann tries another conversation starter
jokes.punchline: Kid directly insults her
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe comedy. Kids being brutally honest is always funny.

jokes.text: Recently, we engaged in something called a 'group hang.' It was like a date, but there were seven other people there. It was very confusing.
jokes.setup: Chris tries to define his relationship status
jokes.punchline: His confusion about modern dating terminology and group dynamics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - overly analytical about simple social concepts.

jokes.text: Labels can be bad, but they can also be good. 'Warning: Toxic bleach' is a good label.
jokes.setup: Chris philosophizes about relationship labels
jokes.punchline: Uses hilariously literal example of product warning labels
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chris logic - taking metaphorical concept and making it painfully literal. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Okay, I know this text you sent me was a lie and Michael Stipe is not actually here, correct? Correct. That was a lie.
jokes.setup: Andy questions Tom's obviously false text
jokes.punchline: Tom casually admits to the deception without shame
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's gullibility and Tom's shameless manipulation.

jokes.text: I can't keep referring to basketball players as 'Khloe Kardashian's husband and his friends.'
jokes.setup: Tom explains his basketball knowledge problem
jokes.punchline: Reveals he only knows players through pop culture connections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - knows pop culture but not sports. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: One jock, one geek who loves stats.
jokes.setup: Tom describes Andy and Ben
jokes.punchline: Reduces them to simple archetypes
jokes.explanation: Simple but accurate character observations. Tom's reductive thinking is in character.

jokes.text: Your gender equality commission is a real sausage fest.
jokes.setup: Chris observes the commission Leslie organized
jokes.punchline: Uses crude slang to point out the ironic gender imbalance
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation. The phrase 'sausage fest' is amusingly crude for the context.

jokes.text: Oh, there's a lady right next to you. Oh, wait, no. It's just a very beautiful man.
jokes.setup: Councilman claims to see a woman on the commission
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's just an attractive man
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection. The 'very beautiful man' detail saves it from being too obvious.

jokes.text: Round of applause for the girl. She has to leave to get more snacks.
jokes.setup: Milton patronizingly applauds Leslie
jokes.punchline: Assumes she needs to leave for stereotypical women's work
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing institutional sexism. Milton's obliviousness is the joke.

jokes.text: Ouch. Why so ornery? It's not the 7th yet.
jokes.setup: Leslie gets frustrated with the sexist treatment
jokes.punchline: Milton references a menstrual calendar, calling back to Paula's story
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing the problem hasn't changed. Economical and disturbing.

jokes.text: Your shoes are red now. Yay. Hooray.
jokes.setup: Kids show Ron some craft project result
jokes.punchline: Ron's monotone, unenthusiastic response to their excitement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - trying to be supportive but completely deadpan about it.

jokes.text: I believe one problem with hiring women is that they're frail and breakable. Is it possible you're thinking about light bulbs?
jokes.setup: Councilman makes sexist comment about women being frail
jokes.punchline: Leslie suggests he's confusing women with light bulbs
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback. The light bulb comparison is unexpected and perfectly cuts through the sexism.

jokes.text: Or your hip?
jokes.setup: Following the light bulb joke
jokes.punchline: Leslie adds another 'frail and breakable' item - his elderly hip
jokes.explanation: Good escalation adding personal insult to the object comparison. Very economical.

jokes.text: You have one woman, and she's a secretary. And she's the best secretary we got, except for Dan. Dan's awesome.
jokes.setup: Steve defends their hiring by mentioning their best female secretary
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it by saying a man is actually better
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing their unconscious sexism. The enthusiasm for Dan is funny.

jokes.text: What? I love garbage. You ever found a dead body in the trash? Or body parts? Like, a torso or a head? Please say head.
jokes.setup: April expresses enthusiasm for garbage work
jokes.punchline: Her excitement is specifically about finding dead bodies
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment. Her morbid enthusiasm is exactly what we'd expect from her.

jokes.text: What do they say? 'Pick up garbage. Put in truck'? Actually, yeah.
jokes.setup: Dewey mocks Leslie's binders with an oversimplified guess
jokes.punchline: Leslie admits that's exactly what's in there
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Leslie's over-preparation. Her honesty makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No, it's Perkins. Always has been. You seem more like a Hanson.
jokes.setup: Chris admits he thought Ron's name was Hanson
jokes.punchline: Insists Ron seems like a Hanson despite being wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's odd certainty about incorrect assumptions.

jokes.text: Either way, can you help me? I'm out of activities, and they're playing with my land mine.
jokes.setup: Ron asks for help watching the kids
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions they're playing with his land mine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - casually mentioning dangerous items like it's normal. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You know, with us being women and all, you'd think our boobs would get in the way.
jokes.setup: Leslie and April performing well at garbage collection
jokes.punchline: Leslie sarcastically referencing sexist assumptions
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie sarcasm pointing out absurd sexist logic

jokes.text: Is that accurate Mr. Phlegner? Is Councilwoman Knope ahead of the regular schedule for this route? Whatever. No comment.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks sanitation worker to confirm Leslie's success
jokes.punchline: He gives a dismissive non-answer that sounds petulant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing his resentment. The 'whatever' is appropriately childish.

jokes.text: Google Earth... Always taking pics.
jokes.setup: Shauna mentions not having a photographer
jokes.punchline: Suggests Google Earth as an alternative photo source
jokes.explanation: Silly observation about Google Earth that's unexpectedly charming in its absurdity.

jokes.text: Andy, guard him. Eh, he's too big. I can't see over him. It's not fair. I feel like everyone should be the same size.
jokes.setup: Ben tells Andy to guard Tom in basketball
jokes.punchline: Andy's childish complaint about height differences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - wanting fairness in the most unrealistic way possible.

jokes.text: Did I do basketball? Kind of.
jokes.setup: Tom asks if he successfully played basketball
jokes.punchline: Ben's diplomatic but honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Ben's gentle honesty is diplomatic but clear.

jokes.text: Tommy for the dunk! My screen!
jokes.setup: Tom attempts to dunk and presumably crashes into something
jokes.punchline: Apparently damages a screen/monitor in the process
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Tom's overconfidence leads to predictable damage.

jokes.text: But it was actually a blood-hungry witch who was wearing their mom's face like a mask!
jokes.setup: Ann is telling what should be a kid-friendly story
jokes.punchline: Takes a horrifying turn with graphic imagery
jokes.explanation: Great Ann character moment - completely misjudging what's appropriate for children.

jokes.text: What is wrong with you, woman? Sorry. I thought kids liked ghost stories.
jokes.setup: Ron's horrified reaction to Ann's inappropriate story
jokes.punchline: Ann's clueless justification
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat. Ann's misunderstanding of children continues to be funny.

jokes.text: This is Stacy Knoblauch's house. I went to high school with her, and she was super mean to me, and now I know that she dyes her hair. I knew that wench wasn't naturally blonde.
jokes.setup: April looks through someone's trash
jokes.punchline: Takes petty revenge by learning personal secrets
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - using her job for petty personal revenge.

jokes.text: She has to use prescription-strength deodorant. This is the best day of my life.
jokes.setup: April continues discovering embarrassing trash secrets
jokes.punchline: Declares it the best day ever over this petty discovery
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of April's petty revenge. Her disproportionate joy is very in character.

jokes.text: Tom, what the hell are you doing? I'm pivoting. Ben told me to pivot. I told you you can pivot, not that you can only pivot. Stop pivoting!
jokes.setup: Tom is apparently spinning in place continuously
jokes.punchline: Took Ben's basketball advice too literally and won't stop
jokes.explanation: Great Tom character moment - taking instructions too literally. The repetition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I may not know much, but you can't just steal the ball from another player. Hey, aren't you the guy who owns Rent-A-Swag? You suck at basketball, man.
jokes.setup: Tom incorrectly explains basketball rules
jokes.punchline: Kid recognizes him and delivers brutal honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Tom gets recognized but for his incompetence. Kids being brutally honest is always funny.

jokes.text: The patient needs a haircut. Her hair is infected.
jokes.setup: Kids are playing doctor with dolls
jokes.punchline: Invent medical reason for cutting hair
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - making up medical justifications for destructive play.

jokes.text: Ah, easy, breezy, beautiful. That's the covergirl slogan. I didn't mean to say that.
jokes.setup: Leslie describes lifting a heavy fridge
jokes.punchline: Accidentally quotes makeup commercial and immediately acknowledges it
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - her brain is so full of pop culture that slogans slip out inadvertently.

jokes.text: What, you think Joan of Arc needed a hand?
jokes.setup: Someone offers to help with the heavy lifting
jokes.punchline: Leslie compares garbage collection to historical heroism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - making grand historical comparisons to mundane tasks.

jokes.text: I don't know why I thought sneaking up on it would work.
jokes.setup: Leslie is struggling with the heavy fridge
jokes.punchline: Admits to trying to sneak attack an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reveal. The absurdity of sneaking up on a refrigerator is very Leslie.

jokes.text: Did you just say you love Diane? No. I did not. Yes, you did! That is so cute!
jokes.setup: Ann catches Ron in an emotional admission
jokes.punchline: Ron's immediate denial followed by Ann's excitement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ron's emotional walls and Ann's enthusiasm for romance.

jokes.text: For God's sake, Hanson, will you please focus on the larger problem?
jokes.setup: Ron is frustrated with Ann's focus on his love life
jokes.punchline: Still calls her by the wrong last name from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ron picked up Chris's incorrect assumption about the name.

jokes.text: Ron loves Mommy! I love nothing!
jokes.setup: Kids repeat what they overheard about Ron loving their mom
jokes.punchline: Ron's desperate, over-the-top denial
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron character moment. 'I love nothing!' is perfectly over-the-top and quotable.

jokes.text: Are you a ghost? Yes, we are.
jokes.setup: Baker asks if they're ghosts (presumably because they appeared unexpectedly)
jokes.punchline: Leslie matter-of-factly confirms they are ghosts
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist humor. Leslie's matter-of-fact agreement is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: Why you think you can lift it? You are two little canaries. Those garbagemen couldn't even lift it. They are like giant bears. Hold on. What do you mean? Bears are bigger than canaries, yes?
jokes.setup: Baker explains why the fridge is unmovable using animal metaphors
jokes.punchline: Gets sidetracked explaining basic animal size relationships
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with the baker's earnest explanation of obvious animal facts.

jokes.text: Fly away, little canaries.
jokes.setup: Baker continues the canary metaphor from moments before
jokes.punchline: Dismisses them with the animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the baker's metaphor. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Look, all we have to do is get some PCP. You can move anything on angel dust. My cousin Hesen, he beat off five cops on that stuff.
jokes.setup: April suggests a solution to moving the heavy fridge
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests illegal drugs and references violent family story
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - casual drug references and disturbing family stories delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: Can I borrow $1,500? You're not allowed to ask what it's for. Fireworks.
jokes.setup: Tom asks for money but says Ben can't ask what it's for
jokes.punchline: Immediately volunteers the information anyway
jokes.explanation: Great Tom character moment - can't help but reveal his impulsive spending plans.

jokes.text: This is why I love you. Oh. Well, I love you, too.
jokes.setup: Diane expresses love for Ron's caring nature
jokes.punchline: Ron's surprised reaction followed by reciprocation
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment. Ron's surprise at emotional expression is very in character.

jokes.text: And it's trash. It's filled with trash. Maybe there's something on the bottom here. Let me see-- No, just trash.
jokes.setup: Leslie describes April's 'present' to commemorate their victory
jokes.punchline: Keeps hoping to find something meaningful but it's literally just garbage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's optimism being defeated by April's trolling. Her persistent hope is funny.

jokes.text: We've got everything: Dope suits, pocket squares, Gingham shirts, glasses with no lenses, Tiny cardigans, Fedoras... Look at this watch!
jokes.setup: Tom advertises his rental business on local TV
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly specific and ridiculous fashion items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his obsession with fashion accessories is very specific to him.
